# @verevoir/accelerator

The aigency accelerator — the commodity developer toolbelt as an MCP server, plus
the substrate/LLM/telemetry/loop primitives it exposes as a library. Split out of
@verevoir/mcp along the governance boundary (STDIO-523): the governed moat lives
in the private @verevoir/capabilities, which composes on top of this package.

This package is PUBLIC and dual-published:
- npmjs           https://www.npmjs.com/package/@verevoir/accelerator  (default)
- GitHub Packages https://github.com/verevoir/accelerator/pkgs/npm/accelerator

## Install / run as an MCP server

Default (npmjs — no auth):

    npx -y @verevoir/accelerator

MCP client config:

    { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@verevoir/accelerator"] }

From GitHub Packages instead (the registry private @verevoir consumers use), add
to .npmrc and authenticate with a token that has read:packages:

    @verevoir:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
    //npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}

## Tools it registers

Source (over @verevoir/context, cached + tree-sitter indexed): read_file,
list_files, get_repo_tree, grep, find_symbol, code_graph, write_file, edit_file,
ensure_fork, ensure_branch, open_pull_request. Work tracker (over
@verevoir/workflows): list_cards, get_card, create_card, update_card, move_card,
list_columns, list_comments, add_comment. Prefer these over built-in
filesystem/shell tools so the shared read cache + index stay correct.

## Library (subpath exports)

Every compiled module is importable by subpath — @verevoir/accelerator/tiers,
/router, /audit, /metering, /result, /edit, /cache, /graph, /manifest,
/instructions, /loop/evals, /loop/refine, /loop/search, /tools/source,
/tools/workflow. @verevoir/capabilities imports these; the dependency direction
is capabilities -> accelerator (never the reverse), which keeps governance out of
the commodity layer.

## Bins

accelerator / verevoir-accelerator (server), verevoir-card-sync,
verevoir-audit-trace.

## Publishing

Tag a release (vX.Y.Z) and push it; .github/workflows/release.yml dual-publishes
to npmjs (needs the NPM_TOKEN repo/org secret) and GitHub Packages (built-in
GITHUB_TOKEN). Bump the version in package.json first.
